lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: I am a mighty and powerful genie. Why do you come here? Speak now or I'll have to think of something exceedingly nasty to do to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're a genie, grant me a wish.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Not all genies are goody two shoes who go round granting wishes. It's something that my pathetic brother does to make people like him, but that's not my style.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: I like to think of myself more as an evil and scary sort of genie, who people talk about in hushed and fearful tones.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well you seem quite scary.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Why thank you, I put a lot of effort into my terrifyingness.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Wait! Did you say quite scary? I'll have you know I am VERY scary!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm yes that's what I meant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I must be off now.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Yes, run. Ruuuun in fear of.. The crevice genie of Nardah! Muhahaha!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you sitting here in the dark?

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Two reasons really. Firstly, I can see in the dark, so light or dark makes little difference to me.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Lastly, I gather it's normal for powerful terrifying creatures to lurk around in dark caves, so I found this one to lurk in myself, nice isn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very pleasant, the snakes are a nice touch.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Why do I get the sense that I'm not being taken seriously here? Please try to remember I am very dangerous and not to be trifled with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just looking around.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: I don't know about that. I can't have just anybody traipsing in here. I have a reputation to keep you see.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Actually I'll tell you what, you can hang around here for a bit, but be sure to spread the word about what a terrifying experience you had when you ran into... The crevice genie of Nardah!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...Ok I think I can manage that.

sections.title: Attempting to take the lamp on his table

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Hey get your hands off my house.

sections.title: Attempting to cast any spell while in the crevice

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Hey, no casting spells on my house!

sections.title: Talking to him after agreeing to the deal before getting the sole

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Do you have the sole of Awusah, so that we can make our trade?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Well no rush, I quite like the statuette too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, it's not an easy thing to get.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Haha, even a pathetic human should be able to manage to get another human's sole.

sections.title: Talking to him after agreeing to the deal with the sole

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Do you have the sole of Awusah, so that we can make our trade?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I have it here.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Ah excellent, here's the statuette then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So um, why do you need part of Awusah's footwear anyway? I hope you're not going to do some nasty magic on his feet or something.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: I hadn't thought of that. I just read that a lot of the most powerful beings are collecting soles these days. I haven't really got many yet though. You provided me with an opportunity to expand my collection.

sections.title: Talking to him after losing the statue without another sole

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost my statuette.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Well fortunately for you, I am a mighty and powerful genie and managed to reacquire it. I will give it to you for another sole.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will go and get one then.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Great! Another one for my collection.

sections.title: Talking to him after losing the statue with another sole

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost my statuette.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Well fortunately for you, I am a mighty and powerful genie and managed to reacquire it. I will give it to you for another sole.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I have one here.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Ah excellent here's the statuette then.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: It's good doing business with you again.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: I am a mighty and powerful genie. Why do you come here? Speak now or I'll have to think of something exceedingly nasty to do to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm after a statue that was thrown down here.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Ahh yes someone did drop a statue on my head recently. Very nice workmanship, looks quite old and valuable too. I'm surprised someone threw it away!

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: I have accepted it as a gift as they clearly respect my terrible powers. They seek to keep me at bay by giving me valuables.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I can make a deal for the statuette?

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Hmm a deal eh? I think we can come to some sort of arrangement. I'll give you the valuable statuette in return for, let me think ....

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: How about I give you the statue in exchange for the sole of the mayor of Nardah?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Isn't that a little harsh?

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: I wouldn't have said so. It seems a perfectly reasonable thing to request to me. I can think of many things a lot worse I could have asked for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How am I meant to remove someone's soul?

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Oh that's easy. In my experience soles are very easy to remove with just a knife.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok I agree to the deal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way am I agreeing to that.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Ah well it's your loss. No sole, then no statuette.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure they know you're down here.

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Silence cur! They fear and respect me and that's an end to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I can make a deal for the statuette?

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Hmm a deal eh? I think we can come to some sort of arrangement. I'll give you the valuable statuette in return for, let me think ....

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: How about I give you the statue in exchange for the sole of the mayor of Nardah?

lines.speaker: Genie
lines.text: Here, have this.
